content.thinking:
Let me analyze the task. I need to fix three configuration issues in a multi-tenant Spark environment on Kubernetes:

1. History Server PVC Reference - change claimName from 'spark-history-pvc-wrong' to 'spark-history-pvc'
2. History Server Log Directory - change from /wrong/path/spark-logs to /mnt/spark-logs
3. Team A RoleBinding Namespace - change subjects[0].namespace from 'default' to 'spark-team-a'

Let me start by checking the current state of the environment, then apply the fixes.

content.thinking:
Good. I can see all three issues confirmed:
1. History Server PVC is 'spark-history-pvc-wrong' (should be 'spark-history-pvc')
2. History Server log directory is '/wrong/path/spark-logs' (should be '/mnt/spark-logs')
3. Team A RoleBinding has subjects[0].namespace = 'default' (should be 'spark-team-a')

Now let me apply all three fixes simultaneously.

content.text: All three issues are confirmed. Now I'll apply all three fixes.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  statusA=$(kubectl get job spark-pi-team-a -n spark-team-a -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Complete")].status}' 2>/dev/null)
  statusB=$(kubectl get job spark-pi-team-b -n spark-team-b -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Complete")].status}' 2>/dev/null)
  failA=$(kubectl get job spark-pi-team-a -n spark-team-a -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Failed")].status}' 2>/dev/null)
  failB=$(kubectl get job spark-pi-team-b -n spark-team-b -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Failed")].status}' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "Check $i (${i}*5s): team-a=${statusA:-Running}(fail=$failA) team-b=${statusB:-Running}(fail=$failB)"
  if [[ "$statusA" == "True" && "$statusB" == "True" ]]; then
    echo "Both jobs COMPLETED successfully!"
    break
  fi
  if [[ "$failA" == "True" || "$failB" == "True" ]]; then
    echo "One or both jobs FAILED - checking logs"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
input.description: Poll for SparkPi job completion (up to 2 minutes)
